words.meaning: The process of assessing and prioritizing bugs or tasks by importance
words.example: Let's do a bug triage to decide which issues to fix this sprint.

words.meaning: A bug that causes complete system failure or data loss
words.example: A critical bug was found — users' data is being exposed.

words.meaning: A temporary solution that avoids a bug without fully fixing it
words.example: There's a workaround — users can use the mobile app instead.

words.meaning: The effect of a bug on users or the business
words.example: The impact is low — only 2% of users use this feature.

words.meaning: To postpone fixing a bug to a later sprint or version
words.example: We can defer this cosmetic bug to next sprint.

speaking.title: 💬 Speaking — Bug Triage Meeting
speaking.scenario: Weekly Bug Triage with Tech Lead
speaking.context: Your team has a backlog of 8 bugs. You and your tech lead are deciding which ones to fix this sprint. There are 3 bugs in question: Bug A affects the login page — 30% of users report it. Bug B is a visual glitch on the settings page — 5 users reported it. Bug C causes incorrect totals in invoices — only happens when tax rate is 0%.
speaking.prompt: Discuss which bug to prioritize first and why. Consider impact on users, business risk, and effort to fix.
speaking.sample_answer: Looking at these three bugs, I'd recommend tackling Bug C first — the invoice total calculation issue. Even though it only affects users with a 0% tax rate, incorrect financial data is a serious business risk. If customers are being billed incorrectly, that's a legal and trust issue. Bug A should be second priority — 30% of users affected is significant and login issues drive churn. Bug B I'd defer to next sprint; a visual glitch on the settings page with only 5 reports is low impact. Does that prioritization make sense to you, or is there a business context I'm missing?

reading.title: 📖 Reading — How to Prioritize Bugs
reading.passage:
Not all bugs are equal. A good engineering team has a systematic way to prioritize which bugs to fix first. The two key dimensions are severity (how bad is the bug?) and frequency (how often does it happen?).

A bug that crashes the application for 50% of users every time is more urgent than a cosmetic misalignment that 1% of users encounter once a month.

But severity and frequency are not the only factors. Business impact matters too. A bug in the checkout flow is more critical than a bug in the rarely-used admin reports, even if both affect the same number of users.

A simple prioritization framework: P1 (Critical) — fix immediately, it blocks core functionality. P2 (High) — fix in current sprint. P3 (Medium) — fix in next sprint. P4 (Low) — fix when there is time, or defer indefinitely.

Document your reasoning. When you decide to defer a bug, write down why. This creates accountability and helps future teams understand the trade-offs made.
questions: What are the two key dimensions for prioritizing bugs?
Why does business impact matter beyond just severity and frequency?
answers: Severity (how bad is the bug) and frequency (how often does it happen).
A bug in a critical business flow like checkout is more urgent than one in a rarely-used feature, even with the same user impact.

key_phrases.phrase: I'd recommend prioritizing this first because...
key_phrases.meaning: Saya menyarankan untuk memprioritaskan ini pertama karena...

key_phrases.phrase: We can defer this to next sprint
key_phrases.meaning: Kita bisa menunda ini ke sprint berikutnya

items.phrase: This is a P1 — it blocks core functionality and needs to be fixed today.
items.indonesian: Ini adalah P1 — menghalangi fungsionalitas inti dan harus diperbaiki hari ini.

items.phrase: I'd suggest deferring this one — the impact is minimal and we have bigger fires to fight.
items.indonesian: Saya sarankan untuk menunda ini — dampaknya minimal dan kami punya masalah yang lebih besar.

items.phrase: There's a workaround for now — let's document it and fix it properly next sprint.
items.indonesian: Ada solusi sementara untuk ini — ayo dokumentasikan dan perbaiki dengan benar di sprint berikutnya.
